creator: john@jnielsen.net
text:
It wasn't until after I sent in the PR that I realized you already have 
ports/107717 open about the issue. I also didn't look hard enough for the 
supported hardware list. In any case, if you like the UPDATING idea (maybe 
even as a temporary stop-gap measure), here is some proposed text:


The newest version of the driver from NVIDIA dropped support for some older 
cards. From the release announcement: "This NVIDIA Linux graphics driver 
release supports GeForceFX and newer NVIDIA GPUs, GeForce4 and older GPUs 
are supported through the 1.0-96xx and 1.0-71xx NVIDIA legacy graphics 
drivers."

Please check the list at
http://us.download.nvidia.com/freebsd/1.0-9746/README/appendix-a.html
to verify that your card is supported before updating the port. If your card 
is one that is no longer supported, you should list x11/nvidia-driver in 
the HOLD_PKGS list in /usr/local/etc/pkgtools.conf to avoid updating the 
port in the future.



JN
